exemplar.title: Among the rebranding from Twitter to X with its changes in brand identity, company representation, and workforce management, which area has the strongest impact on the brand value of X?

detailedExemplarData.title: Among the rebranding from Twitter to X with its changes in brand identity, company representation, and workforce management, which area has the strongest impact on the brand value of X?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistent and accurate identification of âchangeâ as the key concept
Clear analytical framing in introduction and throughout discussion
Effective use of fishbone diagram to reinforce concept integration
weaknesses: Some descriptive passages (e.g., workforce SWOT) drift away from linking back to âchangeâ
Integration of the key concept is not sustained in every section
Occasional reliance on narrative rather than analytical ties to the concept
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3 is appropriate: the commentary shows good understanding and clear analysis of âchangeâ with organisation-specific examples, but it lacks the fully integrated, sustained analytical treatment required for band 4.

strengths: Uses the full complement of 3â5 supporting documents, each clearly referenced
Documents chosen are relevant to the research question
Appendices include well-labelled figures enhancing clarity
weaknesses: Depth of documents is often minimal (single charts or short extracts)
Limited critical discussion of data reliability beyond a vague asterisk note
Lacks a richer range of qualitative sources or more in-depth primary materials
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student provides the required quantity and relevance of documents, their treatment is generally superficial and lacks depth, aligning more closely with a band 2 than the in-depth range expected at band 3.

strengths: Appropriate selection of SWOT analysis, fishbone diagram and brand-equity theory
Tools and theories are generally applied to the research question
Visual tools (fishbone, SWOT) aid understanding and support evaluation
weaknesses: Application remains largely descriptive without quantitative rigour
No advanced models or financial ratios used to deepen analysis
Opportunities to link theory (brand equity) directly to measured effects are missed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates an adequate (band 3) selection and application of relevant tools and theories, but lacks the sophistication and consistently effective application characteristic of a higher band.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses multiple data sources (revenue, user surveys, stock trends) to inform discussion
Some analytical insights (e.g., labour-cost savings vs. morale) go beyond pure description
weaknesses: Analysis is unevenâsections remain largely narrative with minimal critical evaluation
Data manipulation is limited (few calculations or triangulation)
Missed opportunities to tie percentage changes directly to financial outcomes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there is some analysis, the evaluation is superficial and uneven across sections, fitting band 2 more closely than the âsome analysisâ descriptor of band 3.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with presented evidence
Explicitly and directly answer the research question
Acknowledges limitations and suggests further research
weaknesses: Could be more concise and tightly restate the research question
Limited engagement with broader implications beyond brand identity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion logically flows from the analysis and fully addresses the research question, meriting the top band (3) for clarity and consistency with evidence.

strengths: Logical sequence of sections with clear headings and subâheadings
Table of contents guides the reader
weaknesses: Lacks page numbers in the contents page for quick navigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The commentary exhibits an appropriate and consistent structure throughout, fulfilling the descriptors for band 2.

strengths: All required IB elements are present (title page, word count, declaration, bibliography)
Visuals and appendices are properly captioned and labeled
weaknesses: Broken URLs across lines reduce readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With all mandatory elements correctly included and clear visual presentation, the work meets the criteria for band 2 presentation despite minor formatting issues.

strandSelections.reasoning: The commentary follows a logical sequence matching the table of contents, with clear section headings and sub-headings.  Transitions are adequate, enabling the reader to follow the argument, meeting the descriptor for âappropriate structure throughout.â

The chosen documents are clearly relevant to the research question (user survey on the rebrand, revenue figures, stock-price data, workforce memo, internal cost chart) and provide usable quantitative evidence.  Depth is adequate but not extensive; most are single graphics or short extracts, so the commentary must supplement them with secondary citations.  This fits ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the richer range expected for band 4.

All IB-required elements are present: title page with RQ and word count, declaration, contents page, body within 1,800-word limit, proper bibliography, and labelled appendices.  Visuals are captioned, satisfying the top presentation band.

Five distinct secondary documents are referenced as SD1âSD5 and reproduced/annotated in the appendices (survey chart, stock-price graph, revenue table, interview article, internal memo).  This satisfies the required range (3-5).

Tools are generally applied to the research question: the SWOT section links workforce decisions to strengths/weaknesses, and the fishbone diagram identifies causal factors behind falling brand value.  Relevance is clear but treatment is mostly descriptive; calculations or weighted prioritisation are absent, placing the work in the adequate band rather than demonstrating âeffectiveâ application.

Data used include revenue declines, user-loss estimates, advertising percentage drops, stock-price movements and survey percentages.  These figures are referenced and sometimes graphed, indicating adequate selection and use.  The data set is not wide or triangulated enough to be judged âsufficientâ or âeffective,â and there is minimal manipulation beyond quotation, so band 3 is the best fit.

The analysis moves beyond descriptionâidentifying two specific problems arising from brand identity and evaluating labour-cost savings versus moraleâbut depth is uneven and sections such as company representation remain largely narrative.  Integration across sections exists yet is not âmostly effectiveâ or âsustained,â so band 3 (âsome analysis of research questionâ) is appropriate.

The research question is answered explicitly in both the evaluation and the concluding paragraph.  The student also acknowledges limitations and suggests further research, reinforcing that the question has been squarely addressed.

The student employs SWOT analysis, a fishbone diagram, and references brand-equity theory.  This represents an adequate selection that is appropriate for the question, though not especially wide or sophisticated (e.g., no quantitative valuation models or financial ratios).

The conclusion that brand identity has the strongest impact is logically derived from evidence of user loss, advertising decline and the fishbone summary.  No claims contradict cited data, so conclusions are consistent with evidence and clearly stated.

The entire commentary is centred on X (formerly Twitter); statistics, images and narrative all relate directly to that organisation.  The student analyses how the name change, Muskâs leadership and layoffs affected Xâs brand value, demonstrating a sound, organisation-specific approach rather than generic theory.  Nonetheless, some sections merely describe what happened (e.g., stock-price movements) without fully linking back to brand value, so the integration is not strong enough for band 4.

The student keeps the key concept of change in focus from the introduction onward and repeatedly links each of the three impact areas to the re-branding change.  References such as âthis essay examines the concept of change by evaluating â¦â and the later fishbone diagram show clear analytical thinking centred on change rather than treating it as an add-on.  However, the discussion is not woven through every paragraph; for example, the workforce section drifts into generic SWOT commentary with only brief tie-backs to change.  This level aligns with a good understanding and clear analysis, but not the sustained, fully integrated treatment required for the top band.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly articulated and focused, providing a solid foundation for analysis.
The key concept of âchangeâ is correctly identified and consistently referenced, which frames the commentary effectively.
Quantifying the 32 million user loss is effective; consider linking this directly to percentage revenue decline for clarity.
The use of specific advertising revenue drop (>50%) provides strong evidence; consider naming absolute revenue figures to strengthen impact.
The narrative on Muskâs layoffs describes events clearly; link these actions directly to shifts in brand equity theory.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The stock-price graph from Macrotrends (SD5) is relevant, supporting assessment of investor confidence.
The title page includes all required elements (RQ, declaration, word count), satisfying presentation standards.
The table of contents is well-structured but lacks page numbers for each subsection; adding these would improve navigation.
The clear organization of the contents page establishes logical flow and aids reader comprehension.
Background context on the acquisition and brand value sets the scene well, but it would benefit from previewing the analytical framework.
The introduction explicitly states the concept of change and its relevance, integrating the key concept effectively.
Definition of brand identity is clear, but linking each element directly to its measured effect on brand value would deepen analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The survey data from CivicScience (SD2) is a strong use of supporting evidence, illustrating user sentiment quantitatively.
The discussion on negative reactions is descriptive; strengthen it by tying percentages directly to projected revenue losses.
Figure 1 is well-labelled and clearly referenced, enhancing the reader's understanding of user response data.
Discuss the implications of stock-price trends in terms of brand value and stakeholder perception to deepen evaluation.
The SWOT analysis frames workforce management clearly, showing strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The opportunity section is underdeveloped; include projected productivity metrics or case comparisons to strengthen analysis.
The half-year revenue data is well integrated into the evaluation, reinforcing the argument with current figures.
The asterisk note on data reliability is vague; clarify specific limitations of privately held statistics.
The fishbone diagram succinctly summarizes root causes and supports the final judgment visually.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: To strengthen coherence, explicitly reference key fishbone findings in the concluding discussion.
The conclusion answers the research question directly and acknowledges limitations, demonstrating critical insight.
Consider tightening the conclusion by restating the research question and succinctly summarizing the chosen factorâs impact.
Bibliography entries exhibit a good range of contemporary sources, indicating thorough research.
Formatting of some MLA entries is inconsistent (line breaks, italics); ensure uniform citation style throughout bibliography.
URLs broken across lines reduce readability; reformat entries to maintain hyperlink integrity.
Overall organization remains logical and consistent with the table of contents, guiding the reader through clear sections.
